text.title: Risk Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the risk-management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Track opportunities, not just threats; use symmetric response strategies (Exploit/Enhance/Share/Accept).
rule.rationale: Risk registers listing only threats miss half the picture; an unmanaged opportunity is a threat of lost value.

rule.statement: Every risk must have a named owner (a person, not 'the team') and explicit observable triggers.
rule.rationale: Risks without owners are ignored; triggers convert abstract risks into actionable events.

rule.statement: Set contingency reserve = sum(EMV) of all Accepted threats; management reserve sits on top for unknown-unknowns.
rule.rationale: Accepting a risk without provisioning is hoping, not managing.

rule.statement: Each agent-identified risk must cite a specific quoted source from inputs; reject generic risks without evidence.
rule.rationale: LLMs generate generic risks ('scope creep', 'team turnover') without grounding.

rule.statement: Risk reviews are short (15 min) and frequent (weekly), not 2-hour quarterly drills.
rule.rationale: Quarterly reviews find risks that have already become crises; weekly catches drift while affordable.

rule.statement: When a risk materialises, convert it to an Issue and remove it from the register.
rule.rationale: Keeping it in both places inflates risk count and confuses status reporting.

list.item: Trivial internal task (1 person, under 1 week, no external dependency)
Pure agile teams with strong incremental delivery — use 'top 5' sticky list
Crisis already in progress — switch to incident response; add lessons after
